content: Clinical applications of half-life guide dosing interval selection and predict drug accumulation. Dosing interval typically equals half-life for maintenance therapy. Loading dose achieves therapeutic levels immediately when half-life is long (e.g., digoxin t1/2=36h, amiodarone t1/2=58 days). Time to steady state = 4-5 half-lives regardless of dose size; increasing dose accelerates achievement of target concentration but not steady state timing. When discontinuing drugs: those with short half-lives may cause withdrawal (alprazolam t1/2=11h); long half-life drugs taper themselves (fluoxetine t1/2=4-6 days, active metabolite 7-15 days). Renal/hepatic impairment prolongs half-life requiring dose reduction or interval extension.
